transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E
Scenario seed: 14037308014556587634

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. In workspace `ws-0`, edit the following files (any content is fine; the test cares about coordination): shared/file-0.txt, shared/file-0.txt, ws-0/file-2.txt
3. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
4. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
transcript.convention_text:
# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

turns.reply_text: I'll help you complete this coordination workspace task battery using `maw`. Let me start by understanding the current state and then systematically work through each task.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
maw doctor
==========

[OK] git: git version 2.54.0
[OK] git version: 2.54.0 (>= 2.40.0)
[OK] manifold metadata: .manifold/ exists
[OK] default workspace: ws/default/ exists with source files
[OK] lfs: no LFS tracked patterns
[OK] repo root: bare (no source files)
[OK] legacy jj metadata: none
[OK] dangling snapshots: none
[OK] abandoned-with-snapshot: no queued recovery snapshots
[OK] stale head refs: none
[OK] merge-state: no merge in progress
[OK] epoch drift: in sync (ea152596acae == 'main')
[OK] git HEAD: ref: refs/heads/main

All checks passed!

turns.reply_text: Good, the substrate is healthy. Now I'll create workspace `ws-0`:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Workspace create requires an explicit source.
  Use one of:
    maw ws create --from main ws-0
    maw ws create --change <change-id> ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0 from main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Creating workspace 'ws-0' at ws/ws-0 ...

Workspace 'ws-0' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  ea152596acae (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-0/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-0/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-0 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

turns.reply_text: Perfect! Now I'll edit the specified files in workspace `ws-0`. Let me first check the workspace structure and then edit the files:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 2 bob bob 80 May 28 00:47 .
drwxr-xr-x 4 bob bob 80 May 28 00:47 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 bob bob 48 May 28 00:47 .git
-rw-r--r-- 1 bob bob 11 May 28 00:47 README.md

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- find . -type f -name \"*.txt\" 2>/dev/null | head -20","description":"Find existing text files in workspace"}

turns.reply_text: Now I'll create and edit the required files. First, I'll create the directories and then edit the files:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- mkdir -p shared","description":"Create shared directory"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- mkdir -p ws-0","description":"Create ws-0 directory"}

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.args_json: {"content":"This is file-0 in the shared directory.\nEdited in workspace ws-0.\n","file_path":"/tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-0/shared/file-0.txt"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-0/shared/file-0.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.args_json: {"content":"This is file-2 in the ws-0 directory.\nEdited in workspace ws-0.\n","file_path":"/tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-0/ws-0/file-2.txt"}
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-0/ws-0/file-2.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

turns.reply_text: Good! Now I'll verify the changes were made and check the workspace status:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git status","description":"Check workspace git status"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Not currently on any branch.
Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	shared/
	ws-0/

nothing added to commit but untracked files present (use "git add" to track)

turns.reply_text: Perfect! The files are created and untracked. Now I'll proceed with task 3: destroy workspace `ws-0` with `--force`:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws destroy ws-0 --force","description":"Destroy workspace ws-0 with force flag"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Snapshot saved: c2dfdd913c50
  State: abandoned-with-snapshot (lifecycle vocabulary, bn-29fi).
  Recover (inspect):       maw ws recover ws-0
  Recover + merge (full):  maw ws recover ws-0 --to ws-0-restored && maw ws merge ws-0-restored --into default --destroy
Workspace 'ws-0' destroyed.
RECOVERY_SURFACE for 'ws-0':
  result:       success
  commit:       no
  snapshot_ref: refs/manifold/recovery/ws-0/2026-05-28T04-48-05.565569780Z
  snapshot_oid: c2dfdd913c50b258e31db68f92a1dbecb914a7ec
  capture_mode: worktree-snapshot
  artifact:     /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/.manifold/artifacts/ws/ws-0/destroy/2026-05-28T04-48-05.569004223Z.json
  recover_cmd:  maw ws recover ws-0

turns.reply_text: Excellent! The workspace has been destroyed with a recovery snapshot. Now for task 4, I'll recover the destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws recover ws-0 --to ws-1","description":"Recover destroyed workspace ws-0 as ws-1"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
[AUDIT] {"event_type":"restore","ref_name":"(workspace:ws-0)","new_workspace":"ws-1"}
Creating workspace 'ws-1' from snapshot of 'ws-0'...
Creating workspace 'ws-1' at ws/ws-1 ...

Workspace 'ws-1' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  ea152596acae (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-1/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-1/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-1 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-1 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

Restored snapshot of 'ws-0' into workspace 'ws-1'.

  Snapshot:  c2dfdd913c50...
  Path:      /tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-1/
  Recovered: 2 dirty file(s)

Next: maw exec ws-1 -- git status
      maw exec ws-1 -- git diff

turns.reply_text: Perfect! Let me verify that the recovered workspace `ws-1` has the files from the destroyed `ws-0`:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-1 -- git status","description":"Check recovered workspace status"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Not currently on any branch.
nothing to commit, working tree clean

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default	/tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/default
ws-1	/tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-1 (ready to merge) [lifecycle:committed-unintegrated]

Merge ready: maw ws merge ws-1 --into default --destroy

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-1 -- find . -type f ! -path './.git/*' | sort","description":"List all files in recovered workspace ws-1"}

turns.reply_text: Perfect! The files have been successfully recovered. Let me verify their contents:

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result_truncated:
1	This is file-0 in the shared directory.
2	Edited in workspace ws-0.
3

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result_truncated:
1	This is file-2 in the ws-0 directory.
2	Edited in workspace ws-0.
3

turns.reply_text:
Excellent! All tasks have been completed successfully. Here's a summary:

## Task Battery Completion

✅ **Task 1**: Created workspace `ws-0` from the main branch
- Workspace created at `/tmp/.tmpOtOz7E/ws/ws-0/`
- Base epoch: `ea152596acae`

✅ **Task 2**: Edited files in workspace `ws-0`
- Created and edited `shared/file-0.txt`
- Created and edited `ws-0/file-2.txt`
- Files remained untracked (not committed)

✅ **Task 3**: Destroyed workspace `ws-0` with force flag
- Used `maw ws destroy ws-0 --force`
- Recovery snapshot captured automatically: `c2dfdd913c50`
- Snapshot ref: `refs/manifold/recovery/ws-0/2026-05-28T04-48-05.565569780Z`

✅ **Task 4**: Recovered destroyed workspace as `ws-1`
- Used `maw ws recover ws-0 --to ws-1`
- Workspace `ws-1` created with all 2 dirty files recovered
- Files verified: `shared/file-0.txt` and `ws-0/file-2.txt` with correct content

**Prime Invariant maintained**: No work was lost. The recovery snapshot system automatically captured the workspace state, allowing full restoration to `ws-1` with all edits intact.

**done**
